fusion-apps
***********


Description
===========

Use the Fusion Applications Environment Management CLI to manage the
environments where your Fusion Applications run. For more information,
see the Fusion Applications Environment Management documentation.


Available Commands
==================

* create-refresh-activity-details

  * create-refresh-activity

* data-masking-activity

  * create

  * get

  * list

* email-subdomain

  * create

  * delete

  * get

  * get-email-subdomain-csr

  * get-email-subdomain-dns-config

  * list

  * request-email-subdomain-csr

  * update

  * upload-email-subdomain-certificate

  * validate-and-configure-email-subdomain-certificate

  * validate-and-configure-email-subdomain-dns

* fusion-environment

  * change-compartment

  * create

  * create-fusion-environment-admin-user

  * delete

  * delete-fusion-environment-admin-user

  * generate-extract-details

  * get

  * initiate-extract

  * list

  * list-admin-users

  * reset-fusion-environment-password

  * update

* fusion-environment-family

  * change-compartment

  * create

  * delete

  * get

  * get-fusion-environment-family-subscription-detail

  * list

  * update

* fusion-environment-family-limits-and-usage

  * get

* fusion-environment-status

  * get

* marketing-brand

  * create

  * delete

  * get

  * list

  * update

* microsite

  * create

  * delete

  * get

  * get-microsite-dns-config

  * list

  * update

  * validate-and-configure-microsite-dns

* refresh-activity

  * delete

  * get

  * list

* scheduled-activity

  * get

  * list

* service-attachment

  * create

  * delete

  * get

  * list

  * verify

* time-available-for-refresh

  * list

* update-refresh-activity-details

  * update-refresh-activity

* vanity-domain

  * create

  * get

  * list

  * submit-vanity-domain-validation

  * update

  * upload-vanity-domain-certificate

* vanity-domain-activity

  * create

  * delete

  * get

  * update

* work-request

  * get

  * list

* work-request-error

  * list

* work-request-log-entry

  * list-work-request-logs
